# Reproducer derived from https://github.com/home-assistant/core/issues/123309
# repo: home-assistant/core
# title: Database migration error, foreign key constraint

# Last frames in traceback:
#   /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sqlalchemy/engine/base.py line 1967 in _exec_single_context
#   /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sqlalchemy/engine/default.py line 924 in do_execute
#   /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/MySQLdb/cursors.py line 179 in execute
#   /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/MySQLdb/cursors.py line 330 in _query
#   /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/MySQLdb/connections.py line 261 in query

# Minimal repro (conceptual):

def repro():
    # Conditions described in the issue:
    # ### The problem
    # When updating to 2024.8.0 I received a database migration failure.
    # ### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?
    # core-2024.8.0
    # ### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?
    raise IntegrityError('reproduce: see https://github.com/home-assistant/core/issues/123309')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    repro()
expected_fix_hint: Add UPSERT / handle unique constraint collision; transaction rollback.
